role_summary: Son of Vernon Dahmer Sr., the Forrest County, Mississippi NAACP president killed in the January 10, 1966 Ku Klux Klan firebombing of the Dahmer home; brother of survivors documented by Ellie Dahmer's own catalog entry (entry 36).
ai_reading: Dahmer Jr.'s top semantic neighbor is Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 125), with Robert L. Carter (entry 108) and Clarence Magee (entry 21) close behind. The Magee pairing is locally significant: Magee's testimony is the catalog's other Mississippi-rural-organizing voice in his cluster, alongside the Greene (entry 44), Nathaniel Hawthorne Jones (entry 92), and the family arc from his mother Ellie's page. The Southern Struggle axis position of +0.65 confirms the Hattiesburg anchor. The catalog's recurring family-bearer testimony-pattern (Walter -0.78, Hrabowski -0.81, Sales -1.00, Hill Jr. -0.72) holds with Dahmer Jr.'s +0.41 Strategic Vision reading, the opposite-pole position from the cluster, suggesting his testimony carries the long-arc strategic framing of the family legacy (Ellie's 1992 Election Commissioner victory in the same Forrest County district where Vernon died) more than the immediate-action specificity of the firebombing night itself.
biographical_paragraph: Vernon Dahmer, Jr.'s oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 121 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.

sources.title: Vernon Dahmer, Jr. oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
